message:
refactor(tantivy_ingest): deep cx threading through compute_indexer_lag_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Thirteenth caller-migration tick in the strangler-fig phase.

`compute_indexer_lag_with_cx` in tantivy_ingest.rs had two legacy
storage calls — `storage.health()` and `storage.read_checkpoint()` —
inside a fn that advertises cx-first semantics. Tick 128 added
`health_with_cx` as a trait default, and `read_checkpoint_with_cx`
has been available since tick 74. Both now used to thread caller
cancellation into storage's pre-flight checkpoints.

- storage.health → health_with_cx
- storage.read_checkpoint → read_checkpoint_with_cx

Legacy `compute_indexer_lag` preserved unchanged.

Scope note: tantivy_reindex.rs's 6 legacy storage call-sites (lines
358, 367, 802, 1096, 1313, 1540) are all inside legacy private
helpers (`index_loop`, `index_loop_exclusive`) which already have
matching `_with_cx` sibling helpers (lines 1336, 1562). The
`_with_cx` public methods route through the cx-first helpers. No
migration needed there — verified by locating each legacy call-site
via `(?:pub )?async fn` scan.

Verified via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/
ft-rusticmaple-target, --features asupersync-runtime): 217/217
tantivy_ingest + tantivy_reindex tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
